title: Frank Shoots Every Member of the Gang

jokes.text: Frank, would you like another buttered nipple? / No. The cream is making me gassy.
jokes.setup: Innocent offer of what sounds like a cocktail name
jokes.punchline: Frank's crude, literal response about digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Sets up Frank's crude character - functional setup/punchline with mild shock value

dialogue.line: Frank, would you like another buttered nipple?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: No. The cream is making me gassy.

jokes.text: This is your annual 'take me out and see if you can talk me into giving you more of my money.'
jokes.setup: Others trying to butter up Frank with drinks
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately sees through their transparent manipulation
jokes.explanation: Sharp character moment - Frank's paranoid cynicism proves completely accurate

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: This is your annual 'take me out and see if you can talk me into giving you more of my money.'

jokes.text: I brought my own can of anchovies because the fish in this place is not salty enough.
jokes.setup: Normal complaint about restaurant food quality
jokes.punchline: Frank's bizarre solution of bringing canned fish to add salt
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Frank logic - specific, bizarre, and completely in character

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: I brought my own can of anchovies because the fish in this place is not salty enough.

jokes.text: I think we do a pretty good job of pretending like you're not an animal.
jokes.punchline: Backhanded compliment that explicitly calls him an animal
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical insult disguised as praise - brutal honesty

dialogue.line: I think we do a pretty good job of pretending like you're not an animal.

jokes.text: Is that thing loaded, by the way? / Nah, it's not loaded.
jokes.punchline: Frank's confident assertion that will immediately prove wrong
jokes.explanation: Classic setup for the shooting - dramatic irony building

dialogue.line: Is that thing loaded, by the way?

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Nah, it's not loaded.

jokes.text: You shot us! You shot us! I'm shot in the face!
jokes.setup: Frank's previous assurance the gun wasn't loaded
jokes.punchline: Immediate consequence - everyone gets shot
jokes.explanation: Payoff to earlier setup - shocking escalation that drives the episode

dialogue.line: You shot us! You shot us! I'm shot in the face!

jokes.text: Don't be so dramatic. I just nicked you. It's not like anybody's in any real danger.
jokes.setup: People screaming about being shot in the face
jokes.punchline: Frank dismissing gunshot wounds as 'dramatic'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant understatement - Frank's complete disconnect from reality is peak character comedy

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Don't be so dramatic. I just nicked you. It's not like anybody's in any real danger.

jokes.text: Frank shot us. He shot us in the face. We barely survived. / Awesome!
jokes.punchline: Charlie's enthusiastic positive response
jokes.explanation: Charlie's warped perspective - finds violence exciting rather than horrifying

dialogue.line: Frank shot us. He shot us in the face. We barely survived.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Awesome!

jokes.text: Oh, sorry. You came in all excited. I thought you were bragging.
jokes.setup: Misreading the room about shooting trauma
jokes.punchline: Charlie's honest confusion about tone and context
jokes.explanation: Charlie's social obliviousness - genuinely thought they were proud of surviving

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Oh, sorry. You came in all excited. I thought you were bragging.

jokes.text: Getting shot in the face and surviving is pretty cool.
jokes.punchline: Charlie finds attempted murder 'pretty cool'
jokes.explanation: Charlie's twisted value system - violence as achievement

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Getting shot in the face and surviving is pretty cool.

jokes.text: I don't even care about the money anymore. / Oh, yeah, no, no, neither do I. / I don't give a shit about the money. / Oh, yeah. I do care about the money, though. / Yeah, no, so do I. Absolutely. / It's the only thing I care about.
jokes.setup: Claiming to not care about Frank's money after near-death
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicting themselves within seconds
jokes.explanation: Perfect character hypocrisy - can't even maintain the lie for thirty seconds

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't even care about the money anymore.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah, no, no, neither do I.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: I don't give a shit about the money.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Oh, yeah. I do care about the money, though.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: Yeah, no, so do I. Absolutely.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: It's the only thing I care about.

jokes.text: I'm worried he's gonna kill himself with that gun before he gets old and senile enough to rewrite us into the will.
jokes.punchline: Only worried because it would hurt their inheritance prospects
jokes.explanation: Dark but perfectly logical - their concern is purely mercenary

dialogue.line: I'm worried he's gonna kill himself with that gun before he gets old and senile enough to rewrite us into the will.

jokes.text: We need him to live long enough so he can become fully demented.
jokes.punchline: Hoping for dementia to exploit him
jokes.explanation: Ruthlessly honest elder abuse plan - economically dark

dialogue.line: We need him to live long enough so he can become fully demented.

jokes.text: Of course you don't care, Charlie, because your dad didn't have anything to pass down to you. / No, that's true, but my mom does.
jokes.setup: Charlie being excluded from inheritance discussion
jokes.punchline: Charlie reveals he has his own inheritance coming
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Charlie's tooth jar revelation

dialogue.line: Of course you don't care, Charlie, because your dad didn't have anything to pass down to you.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: No, that's true, but my mom does.

jokes.text: On my mom's family side, I got a huge inheritance coming my way. / Wait, what? How did I not know this? You're gonna be rich? / Yeah. Totally. Yeah. / Well, uh, rich in history and legacy and, uh... teeth, of course.
jokes.setup: Charlie claims huge inheritance, others get excited
jokes.punchline: Inheritance is just teeth in a jar
jokes.explanation: Brilliant misdirection - builds up wealth expectation then delivers absurd reality

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: On my mom's family side, I got a huge inheritance coming my way.

dialogue.speaker: Dennis
dialogue.line: Wait, what? How did I not know this? You're gonna be rich?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Yeah. Totally. Yeah.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Well, uh, rich in history and legacy and, uh... teeth, of course.

jokes.text: Everyone on my mom's side of the family, going back to the Niña and the Pinta and the, uh, Santa Margherita, um, they've all been putting their teeth into a jar when they die.
jokes.punchline: Centuries-old tradition of collecting dead people's teeth
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Charlie logic - treating grotesque tradition as valuable heritage. Ship names slightly wrong which adds to Charlie's character

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Everyone on my mom's side of the family, going back to the Niña and the Pinta and the, uh, Santa Margherita, um, they've all been putting their teeth into a jar when they die.

jokes.text: Frank's gun is a big piece of who he is, right? That's his legacy. That's his history. That's like my jar of teeth.
jokes.setup: Charlie defending Frank's right to keep his gun
jokes.punchline: Comparing deadly weapon to jar of teeth as equivalent legacies
jokes.explanation: Charlie's warped logic - equating violence with his gross inheritance

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Frank's gun is a big piece of who he is, right? That's his legacy. That's his history. That's like my jar of teeth.

jokes.text: Charlie, I got some bad news for you, bud. / What? / You turned 40 a long time ago.
jokes.setup: Charlie expecting to get inheritance at 40
jokes.punchline: Revelation he's been 40+ for years
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's complete ignorance of his own age

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Charlie, I got some bad news for you, bud.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: What?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: You turned 40 a long time ago.

jokes.text: Hey, Ma, did you realize I was over 40? / You are?
jokes.setup: Charlie asking his mother about his age
jokes.punchline: His own mother doesn't know how old he is
jokes.explanation: Sad character comedy - mother-son relationship so distant she doesn't know his age

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: Hey, Ma, did you realize I was over 40?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Kelly
dialogue.line: You are?

jokes.text: Do we have any kind of, like, family heirlooms or anything? / No. / Like, anything of value that our family owned at some point? / No. / What if it's just, like, sentimental? Just, like, proof that we existed? / Nope.
jokes.punchline: Mother's increasingly brutal rejections, ending with denying family existence
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - each question gets more desperate, each answer more devastating

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Do we have any kind of, like, family heirlooms or anything?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.line: Like, anything of value that our family owned at some point?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: No.

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: What if it's just, like, sentimental? Just, like, proof that we existed?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Nope.

jokes.text: Those letters exist? Well, well, who's got them? / Donald. / Donald? Donald who? / Oh... / Duck.
jokes.setup: Learning about family letters, asking who has them
jokes.punchline: Mother says 'Donald Duck' completely deadpan
jokes.explanation: Good timing and delivery setup, though the Donald Duck reference is slightly cheap

dialogue.line: Those letters exist? Well, well, who's got them?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Donald.

dialogue.line: Donald? Donald who?

dialogue.line: Oh... Duck.

jokes.setup: Mac explaining away his mother's confusing response
jokes.punchline: Mother's flat 'yeah' showing she wasn't joking
jokes.explanation: Mac's desperate attempt to save face backfires

dialogue.line: My mom's making a joke. She's-she's really funny. She's got a great sense of humor. Right, Mom?

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Mac
dialogue.line: Yeah.

jokes.setup: Mrs. Kelly going to get the tooth jar
jokes.punchline: She literally tried to extract her own teeth with pliers

jokes.text: You gave away the jar of teeth? To who? / Your sisters. / To my sisters? Those ungrateful bitches.
jokes.setup: Learning the jar was given away
jokes.punchline: Charlie's immediate rage and harsh judgment of his sisters
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Charlie's instant fury over teeth

jokes.text: They moved all the way up to northern New Jersey. They're terrible to you. I'm the one who stayed. I'm the one who takes care of you.
jokes.punchline: His delusional view that he 'takes care of' his mother
jokes.explanation: Charlie's self-serving delusion - thinks he's the good son despite being a mess

dialogue.line: They moved all the way up to northern New Jersey. They're terrible to you. I'm the one who stayed. I'm the one who takes care of you.

jokes.text: Frank, could you stop scratching yourself with the gun, please? / I'm itchy, I'm itchy.
jokes.punchline: Frank is using loaded weapon as back scratcher

jokes.punchline: Frank's dog-like behavior with bizarre cause-and-effect logic

dialogue.speaker: Frank

jokes.text: He'd mow us down like Scarface. We can't come at him like that. We'll spook him.
jokes.punchline: Treating confused old man like dangerous crime boss
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Frank's perceived danger level

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: He'd mow us down like Scarface. We can't come at him like that. We'll spook him.

jokes.text: You don't tell a dog you're taking him to a vet to put him down. You give that dog the best day of his life, and then you turn out the lights when he least suspects it.
jokes.punchline: Explicit comparison of Frank to dog being euthanized
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark metaphor - treating human like pet to be put down. Highly quotable.

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You don't tell a dog you're taking him to a vet to put him down. You give that dog the best day of his life, and then you turn out the lights when he least suspects it.

jokes.text: Why is your mom driving my mom's car? / I don't know. Dude, they got some weird arrangement worked out so your mom can focus on smoking.
jokes.punchline: Mrs. Mac's smoking takes priority over driving

jokes.text: Mrs. Mac, do you mind, like, cracking a window or something? / No. / Why not? / 'Cause it's too drafty. It's unhealthy.

dialogue.line: All you're doing is sticking your hands in teeth and people pay you enough to live like this?

dialogue.speaker: Sisters
dialogue.line: We obviously take our tops off. Like...

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: Obviously.

jokes.text: I clogged the toilet. / What the hell? Gross. / Who is this old lady clogging our toilet? / That's my mommy.
jokes.punchline: Mac's proud introduction after toilet destruction

dialogue.speaker: Mrs. Kelly
dialogue.line: I clogged the toilet.
What the hell? Gross.
Who is this old lady clogging our toilet?

dialogue.speaker: Mac
dialogue.line: That's my mommy.

jokes.text: If there was no TP, what did you wipe with? / The letters. / You wiped your butt with the letters that Grandpop sent Dad and then flushed them down the toilet? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Learning what Mrs. Kelly used for toilet paper
jokes.punchline: Family heirloom letters destroyed by bathroom emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect destruction of Mac's legacy - brilliant gross escalation

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: If there was no TP, what did you wipe with?

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You wiped your butt with the letters that Grandpop sent Dad and then flushed them down the toilet?

jokes.text: This is gonna be the new Kelly family heirloom, and I'm gonna put all my teeth in it and all of Mom's teeth in it and none of yours.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: This is gonna be the new Kelly family heirloom, and I'm gonna put all my teeth in it and all of Mom's teeth in it and none of yours.

jokes.text: You sure you don't want to pop a couple in? You know, you can be a part of my legacy, dude. I feel like you should be.

dialogue.speaker: Charlie
dialogue.line: You sure you don't want to pop a couple in? You know, you can be a part of my legacy, dude. I feel like you should be.

jokes.text: Yeah. There. Take that, lock. / Oh, what the hell, man? / And the door wasn't even locked.

dialogue.speaker: Frank
dialogue.line: Yeah. There. Take that, lock.
Oh, what the hell, man?
And the door wasn't even locked.

jokes.text: You know what our legacy is? Spending our lives in this goddamn bar with a lunatic man who acts like a dog and handles his gun like a maniac!
jokes.punchline: Brutally honest assessment of their pathetic lives
jokes.explanation: Perfect show summary - acknowledging their terrible situation

dialogue.speaker: Dee
dialogue.line: You know what our legacy is? Spending our lives in this goddamn bar with a lunatic man who acts like a dog and handles his gun like a maniac!

jokes.text: If you're gonna be crybabies about it, all right, take my gun.

dialogue.speaker: Frank

jokes.text: Ow! Ow! / Shit! / Shit. / Well, there you have it, Frank. You've officially shot everyone in the bar.
